title: Buttery Aerated British Popcorn Toffee Trifle
description: A classic British dessert featuring buttery, aerated toffee popcorn in a layered trifle form, perfect for meal prep.

ingredients.name: Unsalted pop corn kernels
ingredients.notes: Popcorn must be freshly popped, still warm, and aerated.

ingredients.name: Whipping cream
ingredients.notes: Chilled in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes before use.

ingredients.name: Fresh fruit
ingredients.notes: Strawberries or raspberries, sliced or halved.

ingredients.name: Chocolate chips
ingredients.notes: Melted in the microwave for the top layer.

ingredients.name: Confectioners sugar
ingredients.notes: Dust on top of the dessert for garnish.

instructions: In a medium saucepan, combine the heavy cream, granulated sugar, light brown soft sugar, and butter over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the sugar dissolves and the mixture comes to a simmer.
Increase the heat to medium-high and cook to 240°F, or the soft-ball stage, using a candy thermometer, until the mixture turns a deep amber color.
Remove from heat and slowly pour in the corn syrup and water, whisking constantly to prevent lumps from forming.
Return the saucepan to the heat and bring to a boil, whisking constantly, until the mixture turns a deep amber color and the toffee forms crunchy bits.
Pour the mixture onto a greased surface, such as a silicone mat or a lightly greased baking sheet, and spread evenly to cool and set.

Pop the popcorn using a popcorn popper or by heating 2 tablespoons of oil in a large pot over medium-high heat, adding 1/2 cup of popcorn kernels, and covering the pot with a lid. Once the popping slows to 2-3 seconds between each pop, remove from heat and sprinkle with sea salt.
Place the popped popcorn in a large bowl and drizzle with caramel sauce, tossing to coat evenly.
In a separate bowl, whip the chilled whipping cream until stiff peaks form, then fold in the confectioners sugar.

To assemble the trifle, place a layer of toasted toffee bits in the bottom of a large glass or crystal bowl, followed by a layer of whipped cream, a layer of popped popcorn, and a layer of fresh fruit. Repeat this process until the bowl is almost full, finishing with a layer of whipped cream on top.
Drizzle the melted chocolate chips over the top layer of whipped cream and garnish with additional toffee bits, if desired.
Refrigerate for at least 2 hours before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
Before serving, dust the top of the trifle with confectioners sugar and garnish with additional fresh fruit, if desired.
